revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:酸性泉]]


[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|The "Ōfuki" geyser at {{ill|Tamagawa Onsen|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572582}}
, the most acidic hot spring in Japan]]
'''Acidic Spring''' (酸性泉, Sanseisen) is a classification of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}<nowiki/>based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
labeling system. It is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}}
due to its unique components.
== Overview ==
Acidic springs contain significant amounts of {{ill|hydrogen ions|en|Hydrogen ion|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen ions|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen ions|qq=}}
, often in the form of free {{ill|sulfuric acid|en|Sulfuric acid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfuric acid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfuric acid|qq=}}<nowiki/>or {{ill|hydrochloric acid|en|Hydrochloric acid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrochloric acid|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrochloric acid|qq=}}. Due to their acidic nature, these springs are known for their {{ill|antibacterial|en|Antimicrobial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/antibacterial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/antibacterial|qq=}}effects and their ability to promote {{ill|metabolism|en|Metabolism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metabolism|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metabolism|qq=}}
by exfoliating the skin. However, they may cause a stinging sensation or adverse effects on individuals with sensitive skin. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, acidic springs were used for therapeutic purposes as "finishing baths" (仕上げ湯) in {{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Thermal bath|qid=Q1004887}}(hot spring treatments).
Springs with a {{ill|pH|en|pH|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pH|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pH|qq=}}below 2 are specifically referred to as "Strong Acidic Springs" (強酸性泉), while "Acidic Springs" typically refer to those with a pH of 2 to 4.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels ==
{| class="wikitable"
|+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 |qq=}}
</ref>
! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label
|-
| Simple Acidic Spring || Simple Acidic Spring || Simple Acidic Spring
|}
== Definition ==
Springs containing at least 1 mg of hydrogen ions per kilogram of water.
== Health Benefits ==
''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The benefits associated with acidic springs are primarily linked to their antibacterial properties.
=== Indications ===
* '''Bathing''': In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, effective for chronic {{ill|skin diseases|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|qq=}} .
* '''Drinking''': Beneficial for chronic {{ill|digestive disorders|en|Gastrointestinal disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/digestive disorders|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/digestive disorders|qq=}}

=== Contraindications ===
* '''Bathing''': {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General contraindications|ja|一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=一般的適応症|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=一般的適応症}}, and conditions like {{ill|xeroderma|en|Xerosis cutis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/xeroderma|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/xeroderma|qq=}}in {{ill|elderly|en|Geriatrics|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/elderly|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/elderly|qq=}} individuals.
* '''Drinking''': Not recommended during episodes of {{ill|diarrhea|en|Diarrhea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diarrhea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diarrhea|qq=}}

== Biological Life ==
Acidic springs host unique acidophilic {{ill|bacteria|en|Bacteria|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bacteria|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bacteria|qq=}}
, {{ill|archaea|en|Archaea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaea|qq=}}
, and {{ill|algae|en|Algae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/algae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/algae|qq=}}
. Some archaea and specific {{ill|red algae|en|Rhodophyta|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red algae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red algae|qq=}}(e.g., {{ill|Thermoplasma|en|Thermoplasmatales|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermoplasma|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermoplasma|qq=}}, {{ill|Cyanidium|en|Cyanidium caldarium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cyanidium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cyanidium|qq=}}
) exhibit exceptional tolerance to acidity, with certain species capable of thriving at a pH near 0.
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
{{Onsen|qq=}}
